_comment: Connection-standard ports for organ-scrum-service. SINGLE-op organ: decide() records one standup contribution and advances the daily-scrum state machine (extracted from advance_scrum() + get_current_speaker() in app/services/scrum_service.py). 'inputs' are exactly the keys decide() reads from STATE; 'outputs' are exactly the top-level keys written under decide()'s result.output. Types map to the vendored ./types.json vocabulary (the orchestrator types.json at Data-Flow-Advisory/orchestrator@feat/drift-gate was HTTP-404 to the fleet token on 2026-06-11, so JSON-primitive type names are vendored locally and must be reconciled upstream — see the PR description). The clock value used to stamp the recorded contribution arrives via context.now and is spine-supplied EXECUTION CONTEXT, not an inter-organ port, so it is intentionally NOT listed here (consistent with the connection-standard precedent that context.* is not a port). Nested fields inside contributions / contribution / next_speaker objects are NOT ports — only the top-level keys of decide()'s output dict are. 'turn_order' (the running-order the machine advances over) and 'contribution_text' (the payload being recorded) are the two genuinely-required inputs; every other input has a documented fallback in organ.py, hence required=false.
